results.title: Grip and stance
results.brief_description: Demonstrates the position of the hands on a golf club shaft and shows how finger placement on a club is importance id directing each golf shot. Diagrams the placement of the feet in relationship to the ball and discusses the probable results of shot made from different stances. Inclines demonstrations by Tommy Armour ins stop motion and slow motion photography.

results.title: Grimm's fairy tales. Volume 2
results.brief_description: Presented by Noni Hazelhurst, each of the Grimms fairy tales is brought to life by the use of beautifully illustrated full colour drawings of both the characters and their environment. The stories told in this volume are: Tom Thumb (16 minutes) ; Snowdrop/Snow White (16 minutes) ; Little Red Riding Hood (9 minutes); “Rumpelstiltskin (8 minutes). Each story has been divided into its own segment so the educator can easily find stories that suit individual teaching needs. This tape is not intended to be used for entertainment only.

results.title: Grey paddle power
results.creator_credit: Ivan Gaal, Michael Loftus-Hills, LoftCom and Triple A Productions
results.brief_description: This local documentary focuses on a group of elderly men and women who have embraced canoeing. Most train and belong to the suburban Fairfield Canoe Club where they thoroughly enjoy the recreation as well as the social aspect of the sport. Interviews reveal that the elderly members enjoy the physical benefits and challenges of competing, the camaraderie, and the chance to offer young people guidance.

results.title: Grey funnel line
results.creator_credit: Vincent Donovan, Brian Voce and Australia. Dept. of Defence. Public Relations
results.brief_description: Records a 1974 goodwill cruise by a RAN task force to exotic locations in Africa. Shows sailors exploring tropical Mauritius, and ancient ruins of Persepolis, and observing wildlife in Kenya’s Tsavo National Park.

results.title: Grendel, grendel, grendel
results.creator_credit: Alexander Stitt, Phillip Adams, Hoyts Theatres, TVW Enterprises and Victorian Film Corporation
results.brief_description: An intelligent, witty animation based on John Gardener’s popular novel which retells the Beowulf epic. Originally written in 600AD, the story pits Beowulf, a southern Swedish hero against a terrible monster, Grendel. In this adaptation, as with the novel, the legend is retold from Grendel’s viewpoint, making him a sympathetic, philosophic monster plagued by greedy, warring stupid creatures called men who invade his territory and his serenity. The characters are engaging and individual, coloured in pastels with a refreshing ambience. They are given voice by an eclectic range of performers including Keith Mitchell, Barry Hill and Bobby Bright. This is a highly engrossing story and is recommended for its humanist sympathies.

results.title: Gregory Blaxland
results.brief_description: Outlines the activities of Gregory Blaxland in Australia: his influence in public life, expansion of agriculture and grazing, production of wind and the crossing of the Blue Mountains.

results.title: Greg Norman: the complete golfer. Part I: the long game
results.brief_description: Join ‘the shark’ Greg Norman as he demonstrates the fundamentals of the game such as grip, alignment, the swing, and approach shots, all interspered with winning techniques from Greg’s game.

results.title: Greenhouse
results.creator_credit: William C. Crain and Barr Films
results.brief_description: A young boy’s work to repair a greenhouse he has vandalised helps him top appreciate the beauty of nature and to learn the meaning of friendship and a respect for the property of others.

results.title: Green tea and cherry ripe
results.brief_description: Green Tea and Cherry Ripe tells the story of six Japanese women who married Australian servicemen after the Second World War, their efforts to build new lives in Australia and the challenges they faced in an alien land.
